raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing the denial of my fraud claim regarding unauthorized transactions on my Best Buy Credit Card account.

I discovered the following transactions on XX/XX/XXXX that were made without my authorization : 2 XXXX purchase ( s ) totaling {$520.00} XXXX charge of {$9.00} Transaction date : XX/XX/year> As soon as I became aware of these transactions, I submitted my claim online on XX/XX/XXXX with # XXXX and XXXX. also I contacted customer service and reported them as unauthorized. Another fraud claim was opened, but I was recently informed that the claim had been closed. I was not provided with any explanation, evidence, or findings supporting the decision to deny my claim I received a letter today stating that my claim was rejected because I allegedly did not report the issue in a timely manner and, as a result, they are unable to assist.

However, I am unclear about what reporting timeframe is required. I submitted my claim on XX/XX/year>, which was only five days after the transaction occurred. I believed this was a reasonable timeframe for reporting the issue. 

I have attached a copy of the rejection letter, along with screenshots from the online claims system showing that my claim was submitted on XX/XX/year>. I would appreciate a review of my case and clarification regarding the specific reporting deadline that applies to this type of claim.
